description: Fix graphql() silently ignoring GraphQL partial errors (responses where both data and errors are present), causing real API failures to appear as successes.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/newrelic && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Log or surface GraphQL errors when both data and errors are present
userStories.description: In plugins/newrelic/src/newrelic-api.ts, graphql() at line 46 only throws when `json.errors?.length && !json.data`. When both data and errors are present (partial response), the errors array is silently ignored and partial data is returned as a successful response. This hides real API failures from the AI agent. Log the errors at warn level and surface them in the return value or throw if all returned data fields are null.
acceptanceCriteria: When json.errors is non-empty AND json.data is also present, the errors are not silently dropped
At minimum, errors are logged via log.warn() so they appear in the MCP audit log
Tool results include the error information when partial errors are present
